canonical_question: How do I implement upsert (ON CONFLICT / MERGE) across databases?

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: PostgreSQL 15 added SQL:2003 MERGE (2022-10); PostgreSQL 17 added RETURNING to MERGE (2024-09)

constraints: Upsert requires a UNIQUE constraint or PRIMARY KEY on the conflict column(s)
SQL Server MERGE has known bugs with indexed views and temporal tables
MySQL ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE checks all unique indexes, not just the one you intend
All upsert patterns are subject to race conditions under concurrent writes
PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT requires specifying the exact conflict target
